hero.title: Together we build our children's future
hero.subtitle: We are the voice of the families of Colegio Campestre Santo Domingo Savio. Independent, committed and working for every student.

about_preview.title: Who are we?
about_preview.point1_body: We are a legally constituted non-profit, legally independent from the school, under the supervision of the Cundinamarca Government.
about_preview.point2_body: Founded on May 19, 2023, driven by 16 families who believed in an organized and committed community.
about_preview.point3_body: We support school initiatives without being part of its structure. We are strategic allies for our children's education.

project_teaser.title: The covered court: a shared dream
project_teaser.body: Today our children play outdoors. Sun or rain, recess is the same roofless space. We want to give them a dignified place to run, laugh and grow. We are building a covered sports court at Colegio Campestre Santo Domingo Savio.
project_teaser.image_alt: Covered court project — Colegio Santo Domingo Savio

project_page.title: When it rains, children cannot play
project_page.intro: At Colegio Campestre Santo Domingo Savio, recess is one of the most anticipated moments of the day. But when it rains — and in Anolaima, Cundinamarca, it rains often — children must stay indoors, without space, without movement.
project_page.section1_title: The problem we want to solve
project_page.section1_body: The current courts are open-air. Without cover, rain cancels sports, play, and community time. Children need a dignified space to develop physically, socialize and grow. That space cannot depend on the weather.
project_page.section2_body: Build a covered court that allows students to play in any weather condition. A space that also serves for presentations, community events and activities that currently have no venue.
project_page.cta_title: Would you like to be part of this?
project_page.cta_body: We ask for nothing. We simply share that if you ever wish to add your name to this project, you can do so. Every contribution, large or small, brings us closer to the dream of a court where all our children can play.

legal.title: Legal Framework
legal.subtitle: ASOPADRES CCSDS operates under the Colombian regulatory framework applicable to parents associations
legal.independence_body: Under Article 9 of Decree 1286/2005, ASOPADRES is a legally and patrimonially independent entity from Colegio Campestre Santo Domingo Savio. We are not a school body; we are an autonomous entity working for the education of students.

norms.description: Right of free association — constitutional foundation
Right to education and family participation
Family as fundamental nucleus of the educational process
Parents participation. Art. 9: entity separate from school
Unified Regulatory Decree for the Education Sector
Children's Code — minor images require authorization
European data protection regulation — applies to EU donors

privacy.title: Data Protection Policy
privacy.subtitle: Under Colombian Law 1581/2012, Decree 1377/2013, and GDPR 2016/679

sections.responsible_body: ASOCIACION DE PADRES DE FAMILIA DEL COLEGIO CAMPESTRE SANTO DOMINGO SAVIO, NIT 901.740.513-1, located at Vereda La María, Sitio Los Veleños, Anolaima, Cundinamarca, Colombia.
sections.contact_body: Email: asociaciondepadresdefamilia@ccsds.edu.co — Phone: +57 315 2252936. To exercise your data rights, please contact us through these channels.
purposes: Management of membership and affiliation process
Financial management of fees and contributions
Development of Association activities and projects
rights: Access, update, and correct your personal data
Be informed about how your data is used
File complaints with the Colombian SIC or your local EU data authority
Withdraw authorization and/or request deletion of your data
Access your personal data free of charge
sections.area_body: The Board of Directors of ASOPADRES CCSDS, through the official email asociaciondepadresdefamilia@ccsds.edu.co.
sections.procedure_body: Send a written request to asociaciondepadresdefamilia@ccsds.edu.co stating: full name, ID number, type of request (access, rectification, deletion or objection) and description. Response time: up to 10 business days for queries and 15 business days for claims.
sections.validity_body: This policy is effective from its publication date. Any changes will be communicated via official email and published on this site at least 10 business days in advance.
sections.minors_body: Student data for minors is considered sensitive data under Art. 7 of Law 1581/2012. Processing requires explicit authorization from a parent or guardian. Images of minors will only be published with signed authorization from their legal representative, per Art. 12 of Law 1098/2006.
sections.gdpr_body: If you access this site from the European Union, your data is processed under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR 2016/679). As an EU data subject, you have the right to data portability, erasure ('right to be forgotten'), and objection to processing. To exercise these rights, contact us at asociaciondepadresdefamilia@ccsds.edu.co. Our data is stored on servers located in the European Union (Frankfurt region).

problema.title: When it rains, the children can't play.
problema.lead: It rains often in Anolaima. The courts at Colegio Campestre Santo Domingo Savio are open-air. A single rainfall cancels recess, sports, and time together for 200 students. The children need a dignified space that doesn't depend on the weather.
problema.stat1_text: Students without a covered space for recreation and physical education.
problema.stat2_text: Days of rain per year in Anolaima — more than half of the school calendar.
problema.stat3_text: Recess is the first social space of the day. When it rains, it's cancelled.

proyecto.lead: A space that can also be used for theater, music, community events and performances — where the 200 students of CCSDS can grow, regardless of the weather.

donar.legal2: All contributions are recorded in General Assembly minutes and published on this site.

hero.title: A covered court where everyone can play.
hero.lead: The current space at Colegio Campestre Santo Domingo Savio cannot withstand the rain. This is the proposal so that 200 students can have a dignified place for sports, theater, music and gathering — regardless of the weather.

specs.title: A project designed to last for decades.
specs.p1: The covered court is multifunctional: for sports, the arts and the community. It replaces the current open-air space with a structure that allows continuous use throughout the school year and events open to the Anolaima community.
specs.p2: The design respects the rural, coffee-growing surroundings: a lightweight metal gable roof, open facade and a functional rainwater system.
specs.secondary_v: Theater, dance, flag-raising ceremonies, parish events

timeline.title: Four stages, transparent and verifiable.
timeline.lead: Each stage closes with General Assembly minutes published on this site. Contributions are spent strictly in the proposed order.
timeline.step1_body: Topographic survey, architectural and structural design, permits from the Anolaima Mayor's Office.
timeline.step2_body: Launch of the donation campaign. Excavation, footings and base slab. First phase of structural work.
timeline.step3_body: Steel columns, trusses, thermo-acoustic corrugated sheeting, LED lighting installation.
timeline.step4_body: Sports flooring, court markings, bleachers. Inaugural ceremony with the school community and partners.

donar.title: Your contribution builds a roof.
donar.lead: Every amount counts. Choose your currency and payment method. All contributions are recorded in General Assembly minutes.

faq.title: What's worth knowing before contributing.
faq.a1: The General Assembly of ASOPADRES CCSDS, through its Board of Directors. Every transaction is recorded in minutes and can be audited by any member or oversight body. Funds are held in a separate bank account under the association's name (Tax ID 901.740.513-1).
faq.a2: In Colombia: yes, ASOPADRES is a registered non-profit (ESAL) and issues donation certificates. For international donors: deductibility depends on your country and the applicable tax treaty — please check with your tax advisor.
faq.q3: Why $50 million COP and not more?
faq.a3: The budget corresponds to a realistic design for 720 m² using local materials and regional labor. It includes no luxuries or unnecessary elements. It is the minimum amount for a dignified, durable structure.
faq.q4: What happens if you raise more than the goal?
faq.a4: By statutory mandate, any surplus is allocated to maintaining the court and to other projects approved by the Assembly. Nothing is left without a defined use.
faq.a5: Three mechanisms: (1) a quarterly General Assembly presenting all transactions, (2) publication of minutes on this site, and (3) oversight by the Government of Cundinamarca and the Facatativá Chamber of Commerce.
